title: Delicious Ethiopian Shrimp Dabo
description: A flavorful and light Ethiopian dish featuring succulent shrimp wrapped in lamination-puff pastry.
instructions: Peel and de-vein the shrimp, then pat them dry with paper towels.
Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add chopped onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger; cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the shrimp to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes per side or until they turn pink.
Remove the shrimp from the skillet and set them aside on a plate.
Roll out the lamination-puff pastry to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut the pastry into 6 equal rectangles.
Place a few shrimp in the center of each rectangle, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the shrimp.
Fold the pastry over the shrimp, pressing the edges to seal the dabo.
Brush the tops of the dabo with melted butter and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Place the dabo on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each dabo.
Bake the dabo for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
